title: cormus trilobata
intro: Cormus trilobata, also known as the American dogwood or cornelian cherry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to eastern North America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark and fruit, which have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Cormus trilobata include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory issues.
